title: Check Point Harmony SASE Internet Access Policy (Check Point)
spl:
index=proxy sourcetype="cp_log" product="URL Filtering" earliest=-7d
| where action="Block"
| stats count by category, src_user_name, policy_name
| sort -count
description: Harmony SASE Internet Access (formerly Perimeter 81) enforces URL filtering, application control, and content inspection. Tracking policy actions validates acceptable-use enforcement and identifies shadow IT.
value: Security teams monitor Check Point Harmony SASE internet access enforcement — URL filtering and application control blocks — detecting proxy avoidance attempts, phishing campaign impacts, and unauthorized application usage across the remote workforce.
implementation: Forward Harmony SASE logs via Log Exporter. Map URL categories to your acceptable-use policy. Alert on category blocks spiking post-policy change. Report weekly on top blocked categories.
visualization: Bar chart (blocks by category), Table (top users blocked), Line chart (block trend), Pie chart (category distribution).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Check Point Harmony SASE internet access (SWG) events in `index=checkpoint` with `sourcetype=checkpoint:harmony`. Key fields: `action` (Accept/Drop/Reject), `blade` (URL Filtering/Application Control), `appi_name` (application name), `matched_category` (URL category), `user`, `src`, `dst`, `service`.
• Harmony SASE Internet Access = cloud SWG + application control. URL Filtering blocks by category. Application Control blocks by application identity (e.g., BitTorrent, Tor, WhatsApp).

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify internet access events:
```spl
index=checkpoint sourcetype="checkpoint:harmony" blade IN ("URL Filtering","Application Control") earliest=-24h
| stats count by blade, action
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Internet access policy enforcement:
```spl
index=checkpoint sourcetype="checkpoint:harmony" blade IN ("URL Filtering","Application Control") action IN ("Drop","Reject") earliest=-7d
| eval block_type=coalesce(matched_category, appi_name, "unknown")
| timechart span=1d count by block_type limit=10
```

Understanding this SPL: Trends blocked internet access by URL category and application. 'Anonymizer' blocks mean users trying to use VPNs/proxies to bypass filtering. 'P2P' blocks mean torrent usage. 'Phishing' spikes indicate active campaigns.

Application control analytics:
```spl
index=checkpoint sourcetype="checkpoint:harmony" blade="Application Control" earliest=-7d
| stats count dc(user) as users by appi_name, action
| sort - count
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Visit a blocked URL category. Verify block event.
(b) In SmartConsole: Logs & Monitor > URL Filtering/Application Control.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Block trend by type. Row 2 — Anonymizer/bypass attempts. Row 3 — App control blocks.
Alert: When anonymizer blocks exceed baseline.

Runbook:
1. **Anonymizer spike** — Users bypassing SWG. Tighten policy and communicate.
2. **Phishing spike** — Active campaign. Alert users.
3. **Legit app blocked** — Re-categorize or create exception.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No URL Filtering events** — URL Filtering blade may not be enabled in the Harmony SASE policy.
• **`matched_category` empty** — May be an Application Control event, not URL Filtering.
grandmaExplanation: Our internet security system blocks dangerous websites and unauthorized applications. We watch for employees trying to use anonymizer tools to get around the filter, and we track which dangerous websites are being blocked most often so we can warn everyone about active threats.
hardware: Check Point Harmony SASE (cloud), Harmony Connect Client
